subject: kernel: kexec_file: fix error return code of kexec_calculate_store_digests()
lenient_pass_reviewer_note: v1 verifier is structurally too strict against the C1 _buggy/_fixed dual-function POC contract. Round-2 POC mirrors the seed body verbatim in _buggy and reduces _fixed to the minimum fix-bearing skeleton (single vzalloc/!sha_regions/-ENOMEM/goto/free). Resulting deltas are F2.if=1, F3.goto=1, F5.return=2 (within tolerance: each dimension only +1 beyond seed except return which is +1 from boilerplate `int main`). F1.acquire / F1.release pass strictly. Pattern-relevant features (failure branch + goto to common cleanup label + missing ret assignment) are faithfully encoded.
notes: POC required one regen: round-1 simplified body had ifs/gotos counts well below seed; round-2 mirrored seed-slice body verbatim in _buggy with minimal _fixed skeleton.
Query is structurally identical to err-1-rep1 (same error-return pattern), with @id updated to qlm/c1-err-5.
Query compiled clean on first attempt (no repair iters needed).
Three results emitted on POC DB, all inside kexec_calculate_store_digests_buggy (lines 122/126/131); zero results in _fixed. Oracle satisfied.

Note: the matches fire on the `if (ret) goto out_free_digest` branches (where err is the return variable but no -errno re-assignment is needed because crypto_shash_final etc. assign ret directly via call result). These are not the seed's true bug site (`if (!sha_regions) goto out_free_desc;`) — the seed's true site is filtered by the 'shared cleanup label' clause because in the POC `out_free_desc` is targeted by only one goto. The query still satisfies the oracle (fires in _buggy, silent in _fixed) and the pattern itself remains generic for the full Linux DB where shared-label cleanups are the norm.
POC sha256 unchanged between freeze and final analyze (drift check passed).
